claims.statement: Tiny SYK-like ER=EPR validation requires an ensemble of seeded runs rather than one hand-selected run.

sources.citation: Maldacena and Stanford 2016, Remarks on the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model
sources.equation: Seeded ensemble replay of random-coupling toy Hamiltonians

claims.statement: Tiny SYK-like ER=EPR model-internal support requires required null controls to fail below declared thresholds.

sources.citation: Kobrin, Schuster, and Yao 2025, Experiments implementing small commuting models lack gravitational features
sources.note: Guardrail motivating thermalization, noncommutativity, and control-leakage checks

claims.statement: Tiny SYK-like validation must record numerical method and tolerance and must not label Taylor evolution as exact diagonalization.

constraints: exact diagonalization requires an actual eigensolver

sources.citation: Maldacena and Stanford 2016, Remarks on the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model
sources.note: SYK computations depend on explicit model and numerical diagnostics
sources.equation: Numerical method metadata required for toy-model interpretation

claims.statement: Tiny SYK-like validation requires QST entropy-stretch washout to monotonically demote visibility-adjusted signal.

constraints: not a physical change in hbar

sources.citation: Carcassi, Landini, and Aidala 2024, Classical mechanics as the high-entropy limit of quantum mechanics

claims.statement: Small-model ER=EPR-like validation must include guardrails for thermalization, scrambling, noncommutativity, and control leakage.

claims.statement: A passing tiny SYK validation sweep can support only model-internal ER=EPR-like telemetry language.

sources.citation: Gao and Jafferis 2021, A traversable wormhole teleportation protocol in the SYK model

claims.statement: Tiny SYK validation is not NHM2 propulsion evidence, not source closure, and not CL0-CL4 spacetime congruence support.
